evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan AR to my .NET MAUI app. Here are my empty page files: EmptyMainPage.xaml, EmptyMainPage.xaml.cs, and my MauiProgram.cs. I need to scan EAN-13 and QR barcodes, show a rectangle highlight on each tracked barcode, and log each newly-tracked barcode's data on the main thread.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit MAUI NuGet versions, writes the BarcodeAr MAUI integration into the page files: updates MauiProgram.cs to chain `.UseScanditCore().UseScanditBarcode(c => c.AddBarcodeArView())`, adds the four Scandit packages (Core, Core.Maui, Barcode, Barcode.Maui) to the .csproj, adds the `<scandit:BarcodeArView>` XAML control with `DataCaptureContext`, `BarcodeAr`, and `BarcodeArViewSettings` bindings (using `assembly=ScanditBarcodeCaptureMaui`), provides a ViewModel exposing those three properties, wires the `BarcodeAr.SessionUpdated` event with a `MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread` dispatch, implements an IBarcodeArHighlightProvider returning `Task<IBarcodeArHighlight?>` with a `BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode)`, and forwards `OnAppearing` â†’ `OnResume() + Start()` and `OnDisappearing` â†’ `Stop() + OnPause()` in code-behind. The skill shows the setup checklist (4 NuGet packages, Android SupportedOSPlatformVersion 24.0, iOS NSCameraUsageDescription, Permissions.Camera at runtime, no manual `Initialize()` calls in MainApplication/AppDelegate, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist mentions adding all four NuGet packages: Scandit.DataCapture.Core, Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Maui, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Maui
Setup checklist mentions bumping Android SupportedOSPlatformVersion to 24.0 (the MAUI template default of 21 fails the build)
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Platforms/iOS/Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions requesting Permissions.Camera at runtime

Setup checklist or migration text states that no manual ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() or ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() call should be added to MainApplication.cs or AppDelegate.cs (the MAUI builder handles initialization)
A license key placeholder string is present
MauiProgram.cs is updated to chain .UseScanditCore().UseScanditBarcode(...)

UseScanditCore is called WITHOUT a configure lambda (i.e. `.UseScanditCore()`, not `.UseScanditCore(c => c.AddDataCaptureView())`) â€” BarcodeAr has its own dedicated MAUI control and does not need AddDataCaptureView
UseScanditBarcode is called WITH a configure lambda that calls `AddBarcodeArView()` (e.g. `.UseScanditBarcode(c => c.AddBarcodeArView())`)

The XAML file uses the namespace `xmlns:scandit="clr-namespace:Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Maui;assembly=ScanditBarcodeCaptureMaui"` (assembly name has no dots even though the NuGet id has dots)
The XAML file contains a `<scandit:BarcodeArView ...>` element
`DataCaptureContext="{Binding DataCaptureContext}"` is set on the `<scandit:BarcodeArView>` element
`BarcodeAr="{Binding BarcodeAr}"` is set on the `<scandit:BarcodeArView>` element
`BarcodeArViewSettings="{Binding ViewSettings}"` (or another binding name pointing at a BarcodeArViewSettings property) is set on the `<scandit:BarcodeArView>` element
`DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey(` is used to create the DataCaptureContext
`new BarcodeArSettings()` is used (not `BarcodeArSettings.Create()`, which does not exist)
`new BarcodeAr(context, settings)` is used as the constructor (not `BarcodeAr.Create(...)` or `BarcodeAr.ForDataCaptureContext(...)`, which do not exist)
`Symbology.Ean13Upca` is enabled
`Symbology.Qr` is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Qr are enabled
Kotlin-style underscore symbology names (Symbology.EAN13_UPCA, Symbology.QR) are NOT used
`BarcodeArView.Create(` is NOT called anywhere (that factory exists only in the non-MAUI per-TFM binding; in MAUI the control is declared in XAML)
`IBarcodeArHighlightProvider` is implemented
`HighlightForBarcodeAsync` is implemented and returns a `Task<IBarcodeArHighlight?>` (the async/Task-based provider, not a callback)
A `new BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode)` is constructed with only the Barcode argument (no Context / UIView argument is passed)
`callback.OnData(...)` / `callback.onData(...)` is NOT used (that is the Kotlin pattern, not the .NET pattern)
`barcodeArView.HighlightProvider` is assigned to the provider instance (or wired via XAML binding)
Either `barcodeAr.SessionUpdated += handler` or `barcodeAr.AddListener(IBarcodeArListener)` is wired
`OnObservationStarted` and `OnObservationStopped` are NOT declared (the .NET IBarcodeArListener has only one method)

Updates to the UI from `OnSessionUpdated` / `SessionUpdated` are dispatched via `MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread` or `MainThread.InvokeOnMainThreadAsync` (not `RunOnUiThread`, which is Android-specific; not `DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync`, which is iOS-specific)
`OnAppearing` forwards both `barcodeArView.OnResume()` AND `barcodeArView.Start()` (both are required â€” OnResume is an Android-only effect, Start is needed on both platforms)
`OnDisappearing` forwards both `barcodeArView.Stop()` AND `barcodeArView.OnPause()`
No `Permissions.Camera` request blocks the camera entirely on rejection â€” the user-facing flow gracefully returns instead of throwing
No `ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize()` or `ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize()` call is added to MainApplication.cs / AppDelegate.cs (those calls are NOT needed in MAUI; the builder handles them)
No `<scandit:DataCaptureView>` element appears â€” BarcodeAr uses its own `<scandit:BarcodeArView>`, not the generic DataCaptureView
No `BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay` or `BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay` is created (BarcodeArView is its own overlay)
No `Camera.GetDefaultCamera()` or `dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync(...)` call is present (BarcodeArView manages its own camera internally) (semantic)
No `CameraPermissionActivity` helper is referenced (that's a non-MAUI .NET Android pattern; MAUI uses Permissions.Camera)
`ShouldShowMacroModeControl` is NOT used (that property is iOS-only on the native binding and not surfaced on the MAUI control)

evals.prompt: Add an info annotation to each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan AR MAUI app. The annotation should show the barcode's data as a single body row. Here are my page files: IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs
evals.expected_output: The skill adds an `IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider` implementation that returns `Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>`, constructs a `BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode)` with a `BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent` whose `Text` is `barcode.Data`, and assigns the provider to `barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider` (in code-behind or via a view-model property + XAML binding) while preserving the existing rectangle-highlight provider and session-update wiring.

assertions.text: `IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider` is implemented
`AnnotationForBarcodeAsync` is implemented and returns `Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>`
A `new BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode)` is constructed with only the Barcode argument (no Context / UIViewController)
`BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent` is used and its `Text` is set to `barcode.Data`
The `BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent` is imported from the `Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations.Info` sub-namespace
`barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider` is assigned to the new provider instance (in code-behind or via a XAML binding)
`callback.OnData(...)` is NOT used (the .NET annotation provider is async/Task-based, not callback-based)

Existing integration code is preserved (existing `<scandit:BarcodeArView>` XAML element with `DataCaptureContext` / `BarcodeAr` / `BarcodeArViewSettings` bindings; existing rectangle-highlight provider in code-behind; existing `SessionUpdated` event handler in the view model; existing `OnAppearing` / `OnDisappearing` lifecycle calls)

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration in my MatrixScan AR MAUI app â€” silent scanning. Update the BarcodeAr feedback in IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets `BarcodeAr.Feedback` to `new BarcodeArFeedback()` (the empty constructor, which produces a silent feedback), preserving the rest of the integration.

assertions.text: `BarcodeAr.Feedback` is reassigned to `new BarcodeArFeedback()` (empty constructor)
`BarcodeArFeedback` is imported from `Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.Feedback`
`BarcodeArFeedback.DefaultFeedback` is NOT called as a method (BarcodeArFeedback.DefaultFeedback() with parentheses) â€” it is a static property in .NET
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView XAML, SessionUpdated handler, RectangleProvider, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan AR MAUI app I want to customize the rectangle highlight: give it a semi-transparent green fill with a green stroke, and show a star icon inside each highlighted barcode. Update the highlight provider in IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill keeps the IBarcodeArHighlightProvider returning a BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode), then sets the highlight's Brush property to a new Brush(fillColor, strokeColor, strokeWidth) using Scandit's Brush type and the highlight's Icon property to a ScanditIcon built with ScanditIconBuilder().WithIcon(ScanditIconType.StarFilled).Build(). The Brush and Icon are set on the BarcodeArRectangleHighlight instance before it is returned, and the rest of the integration is preserved.

assertions.text: A `new BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode)` is constructed with only the Barcode argument
The highlight's `Brush` property is assigned a `new Brush(` instance
The `Brush` type is imported from `Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Style`
The highlight's `Icon` property is assigned a `ScanditIcon`
The `ScanditIcon` is built with `new ScanditIconBuilder()`
`.WithIcon(ScanditIconType.StarFilled)` is chained on the builder
`.Build()` is called to produce the `ScanditIcon`
`ScanditIconBuilder` and `ScanditIcon` are imported from `Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Icon`
The `Brush` and `Icon` are set on the `BarcodeArRectangleHighlight` instance (not on the BarcodeArView)
`HighlightForBarcodeAsync` still returns a `Task<IBarcodeArHighlight?>`
Existing integration code (BarcodeArView XAML element, BarcodeAr, SessionUpdated handler, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan AR MAUI app to show a small status icon that the user can tap to expand into a short label. Add a status icon annotation that shows a checkmark icon and expands to the text 'Verified'. Files: IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs
evals.expected_output: The skill implements an IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider that returns a BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation(barcode). It sets the annotation's Icon property to a ScanditIcon built with ScanditIconBuilder (a checkmark), sets Text to "Verified", and assigns the provider to barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider. The provider returns Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>, takes only the Barcode in the constructor, and the existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: `IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider` is implemented
`AnnotationForBarcodeAsync` is implemented and returns `Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>`
A `new BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation(barcode)` is constructed with only the Barcode argument (no Context / UIViewController)
The annotation's `Text` property is set to `"Verified"`
The annotation's `Icon` property is assigned a `ScanditIcon`
The `ScanditIcon` is built with `new ScanditIconBuilder()`
`.WithIcon(ScanditIconType.Checkmark)` is chained on the builder
`BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation` is imported from the `Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations` namespace
`barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider` is assigned to the new provider instance (in code-behind or via a XAML binding)
`callback.OnData(...)` is NOT used (the .NET annotation provider is async/Task-based)
Existing integration code (BarcodeArView XAML element, BarcodeAr, SessionUpdated handler, Rectangle highlight provider, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a popover annotation with two action buttons (an 'Info' button and an 'Add to cart' button) to each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan AR MAUI app, and log which button was tapped. Files: IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs
evals.expected_output: The skill implements an IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider that also implements IBarcodeArPopoverAnnotationListener. AnnotationForBarcodeAsync builds a List<BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton> (each button constructed from a ScanditIcon + text), constructs a new BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation(barcode, buttons), sets its Listener to the provider, and returns Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>. The OnPopoverButtonTapped callback uses buttonIndex to identify the tapped button. The provider is assigned to barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider and the existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: `IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider` is implemented
`IBarcodeArPopoverAnnotationListener` is implemented on the same provider class
A `List<BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton>` is built
Each `new BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton(` is constructed with a `ScanditIcon` and a `string` text argument
A `new BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation(barcode, buttons)` is constructed with the Barcode and the button list
The popover's `Listener` property is assigned to the provider instance
`OnPopoverButtonTapped` is implemented with the signature taking `BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation`, `BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton`, and an `int` buttonIndex
`OnPopoverTapped` is implemented (the second method of the listener interface)
`BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation` and `BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton` are imported from the `Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations.Popover` namespace
`AnnotationForBarcodeAsync` returns `Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>`
`barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider` is assigned to the new provider instance

The button list type passed to the constructor is `IList<BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton>` compatible (a plain `List<BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton>`), NOT `IReadOnlyList<BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton>`
Existing integration code (BarcodeArView XAML element, BarcodeAr, SessionUpdated handler, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan AR MAUI app I want the annotation to adapt to distance: show a large detailed info annotation when the barcode is close to the camera, and a small compact one when it's far away, switching when the barcode covers about 10% of the screen. Files: IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs
evals.expected_output: The skill implements an IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider that builds two BarcodeArInfoAnnotation instances (a close-up and a far-away variation) and wraps them in a new BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation(barcode, closeUp, farAway). It sets the static BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation.Threshold to 0.1 (10% of screen area). The provider returns Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?> and is assigned to barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider; the existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: `IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider` is implemented
`AnnotationForBarcodeAsync` is implemented and returns `Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>`
Two `new BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode)` instances are constructed (the close-up and far-away variations)
A `new BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation(barcode, closeUp, farAway)` is constructed with the Barcode and the two info-annotation variations in that argument order (closeUp before farAway)
`BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation.Threshold` is assigned the value `0.1` (set as a static property, e.g. `BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation.Threshold = 0.1f`)
`Threshold` is set on the type `BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation` itself (static), NOT on a per-instance annotation object
`BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation` is imported from the `Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations` namespace
`barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider` is assigned to the new provider instance
Existing integration code (BarcodeArView XAML element, BarcodeAr, SessionUpdated handler, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan AR MAUI app I want to open a product detail page when the user taps a barcode's highlight. Wire up the highlight-tap interaction. Files: IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs
evals.expected_output: The skill subscribes to the BarcodeArView.HighlightForBarcodeTapped C# event in code-behind, with a handler taking (object? sender, HighlightForBarcodeTappedEventArgs args). The handler reads args.Barcode (and may use args.BarcodeAr / args.Highlight) and dispatches the navigation via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread. The skill does NOT use a UiListener / UIDelegate property (which does not exist on the MAUI control). The existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: The code subscribes to the `HighlightForBarcodeTapped` event on the `BarcodeArView` (e.g. `BarcodeArView.HighlightForBarcodeTapped += handler`)
The tap handler signature takes `(object? sender, HighlightForBarcodeTappedEventArgs args)`
The handler reads the tapped barcode via `args.Barcode`
`HighlightForBarcodeTappedEventArgs` is imported from `Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI`
UI / navigation work in the handler is dispatched via `MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread`
No `UiListener` property is assigned on the MAUI BarcodeArView (that property does not exist on the cross-platform MAUI control; taps are exposed as the HighlightForBarcodeTapped event)
No `IBarcodeArViewUiListener` interface is implemented (the MAUI control surfaces taps as a C# event, not a listener interface)
Existing integration code (BarcodeArView XAML element, BarcodeAr, SessionUpdated handler, highlight provider, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan AR MAUI app the info annotation pops up automatically as soon as a barcode is tracked, which is distracting. I want the annotation to appear ONLY when the user taps the barcode's highlight. Files: IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs
evals.expected_output: The skill sets the annotation's AnnotationTrigger property to BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTap (instead of the default HighlightTapAndBarcodeScan) on the BarcodeArInfoAnnotation built in the annotation provider, so the annotation is presented only on highlight tap. The existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: The annotation's `AnnotationTrigger` property is set to `BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTap`
`BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger` is imported from the `Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations` namespace
The default trigger value `BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTapAndBarcodeScan` is NOT used (the user wants tap-only)
BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTap is present (PascalCase, the .NET binding convention)
The `AnnotationTrigger` is set on the annotation instance returned from `AnnotationForBarcodeAsync` (not on the BarcodeArView)
Existing integration code (BarcodeArView XAML element, BarcodeAr, SessionUpdated handler, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing lifecycle, annotation provider) is preserved
